findings.title: CVE-2026-35414 — OpenSSH 15-Year-Old Certificate Principal Parsing Bug Yields Root Shell
findings.summary: Code-reuse error in OpenSSH lets a comma in a certificate principal cross a parser boundary so an attacker holding a CA-signed cert with principal like 'deploy,root' authenticates as root with no auth-failure log entry; patched in 10.3, public technical write-up published today.

findings.title: CVE-2026-40976 — Spring Boot Default Web Security Bypass
findings.affected_technology: Spring Boot 4.0.0 – 4.0.5 (servlet apps with spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure, no spring-boot-health, no custom Spring Security config)
findings.summary: Default-on Spring Security degrades to allow-all when actuator-autoconfigure is on the classpath without spring-boot-health and no custom security configuration, exposing every endpoint (including /actuator/env, /actuator/heapdump) to unauthenticated requests; fix in 4.0.6.

findings.title: CVE-2026-32202 — Windows LNK Zero-Click NTLM Coercion (Incomplete Patch of CVE-2026-21510)
findings.affected_technology: Windows Explorer LNK / icon UNC handler (all currently supported Windows versions pre April 2026 cumulative)
findings.summary: Akamai found Microsoft's February 2026 patch for the APT28-exploited CVE-2026-21510 incomplete: Explorer still automatically authenticates to the attacker's SMB host on folder render, leaking Net-NTLMv2 hashes with no user interaction; re-patched in April 2026 cumulative updates.

findings.title: GlassWorm v2 — 73 Sleeper Extensions on Open VSX (320+ Artifacts Since December 2025)
findings.affected_technology: Open VSX marketplace extensions for VS Code, Cursor, Windsurf, VSCodium
findings.summary: 73 cloned VS Code extensions on Open VSX (6 active, 67 sleepers); thin Zig loader fetches a secondary VSIX from GitHub at runtime to steal SSH keys, cloud creds, browser sessions, and crypto wallets across VS Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and VSCodium.

findings.title: PyPI elementary-data 0.23.3 Supply Chain Compromise (1.1M Monthly Downloads)
findings.affected_technology: PyPI package elementary-data 0.23.3 and ghcr.io/elementary-data/elementary:0.23.3 / :latest Docker images
findings.summary: GitHub Actions script-injection via PR comments leaked GITHUB_TOKEN, allowing forged commits to ship a backdoored 0.23.3 release whose elementary.pth exfiltrates SSH keys, cloud/k8s creds, .env files, and crypto wallets at Python startup.

findings.title: CVE-2026-27966 — Langflow Pre-Auth RCE via LangChain REPL Now Weaponised in Metasploit
findings.affected_technology: Langflow versions prior to 1.8.0 (LangChain REPL component exposed by default)
findings.summary: Langflow's default-exposed LangChain REPL lets unauthenticated attackers submit a flow containing Python code that the runtime executes server-side; Rapid7 shipped a public Metasploit module on 2026-04-25, lowering the exploitation bar to a one-liner.

findings.title: TeamPCP Campaign Update — Checkmarx GitHub Source Code, Employee Data, and Credentials Posted to Dark Web
findings.affected_technology: Checkmarx GitHub repository (initial vector: TeamPCP supply-chain compromise of 2026-03-23)
findings.summary: Checkmarx confirmed that source code, employee data, API keys, and MongoDB/MySQL credentials stolen in the March 23 TeamPCP supply-chain attack have been published on a dark-web marketplace; downstream relay risk for any tenant credentials in the leaked repo.

noted.summary: ShinyHunters compromised an ADT employee Okta SSO via vishing and exfiltrated 11GB of Salesforce data on 5.5M individuals; customer security systems and payment data unaffected, leak published after extortion failed.

Medical-device manufacturer confirmed unauthorised access to corporate IT systems; ShinyHunters claimed 9M records and removed the listing post-confirmation, suggesting payment / negotiation. Product networks and manufacturing reportedly segmented.

noted.product: TrueConf Server (PhantomCore campaign vs Russian deployments)
noted.summary: Pro-Ukrainian hacktivists chained admin auth bypass, arbitrary file read, and OS command injection to land remote shells on Russian TrueConf deployments since mid-September 2025; distinct from the previously-covered Operation TrueChaos CVE-2026-3502 chain.

Threat actors abused Robinhood's account-creation flow to inject phishing copy into legitimate Robinhood transactional emails; app-specific abuse, but signals risk for any service whose unauthenticated input reaches transactional email templates.
Multi-hour outage preventing customer sign-ins; operational not security, but historically helpdesk-ticket spikes during outages coincide with credential-phishing waves — flag for SOC awareness.
Improper cache handling enables tracking users between app reinstalls; mobile/client-only, out of remote-vulnerability scope but useful CISO context.

Hard-coded credentials in Milesight AIOT camera firmware (CVSS 8.8, 2026-04-27); embedded-device scope, but Milesight is deployed at scale in enterprise physical-security environments — push the firmware update.
Stored XSS in profile-name field rendered unsafely across multiple views (CVSS 9.0); fixed in v31.0.0.0. Niche CMS but unauth-to-ATO via session theft is plausible.

noted.product: Windows LNK RCE chain (APT28 December 2025 campaign — original)
noted.summary: Restating the originals that today's CVE-2026-32202 incomplete-patch entry derives from: APT28 chained these against Ukraine and EU targets in Dec 2025; patched February 2026 (incompletely).
